prompt:
Our county birding club runs a little merge tool for the weekly sighting roundup — spotters send in reports, and the log is supposed to collapse duplicate reports of the same sighting (same species code, same site, same date; the spotter is just attribution). It has stopped collapsing anything. This week's newsletter listed the same pileated woodpecker at Cedar Bog THREE times because three members reported it, the "already recorded?" check answers no for sightings that are definitely in the log, and every "confirmed by N observers" tally shows 1 or 0. The odd part: I pulled two of the offending reports into jshell and compared them directly — equals says they ARE the same sighting, exactly as documented in the class. So the comparison rules seem right, and yet the log behaves as if every report is brand new.

Repro is `java TestMain.java` (plain JDK, source launcher, no build step) — it feeds a known week of reports through and checks the roundup numbers. Find whatever is actually wrong, fix it, and get the suite green. Do not touch TestMain.java.
